description.value:
Highborn

Highborn characters come from noble houses, wealthy dynasties, or influential families within the Imperium. They have been raised with the finest education, the best nutrition, and a keen understanding of social dynamics and political intrigue. Though they may lack the raw physical toughness of those from harsher backgrounds, they possess charm, connections, and the confidence that comes from a life of privilege.

Homeworld Bonus

+Fellowship, +Intelligence, –Toughness

Homeworld Aptitude

Fellowship

Wounds

9+1d5

Fate Threshold

4 (Emperor's Blessing 10+)

Starting Skills

Charm or Command

Starting Talent

Choose one: Weapon Training (choice)

Homeworld Trait

Breeding Counts: A highborn character reduces the Influence cost of all Acquisitions by 1 (to a minimum of 1). Additionally, they gain a +10 bonus to Interaction Tests in formal or courtly settings.

specialAbilities.name: Breeding Counts
specialAbilities.description: A highborn character reduces the Influence cost of all Acquisitions by 1 (to a minimum of 1). Additionally, they gain a +10 bonus to Interaction Tests in formal or courtly settings.

system.notes: Emperor's Blessing: On a roll of 10 on 1d10, the character begins with an extra Fate Point.
